full_prompt: Design a reinforced concrete trunnion bearing pedestal for the feed-end of a 10.4 m diameter x 5.2 m long gearless semi-autogenous grinding (SAG) mill supporting a hard-rock copper flowsheet at 60,000 t per day throughput. The feed-end pedestal is a single reinforced concrete block 5.0 m long x 4.0 m wide x 3.0 m tall poured in normal-weight concrete fc' 34.5 MPa with ASTM A615 Grade 60 rebar. The pedestal supports half of the 1,250 t operating mass of the charged mill (charged with 40% ball filling by volume plus ore), giving a vertical static reaction of 6,130 kN at the feed-end bearing plus a horizontal reaction of 1,550 kN from drive torque transmitted through the mill shell at a drive power of 12.5 MW driving the mill at 9.5 rpm (0.158 Hz). Loads: (LC1) 6,130 kN vertical static bearing reaction, (LC2) 1,550 kN horizontal torque reaction, (LC3) a dynamic amplitude of 400 kN vertical and 200 kN horizontal at 0.158 Hz from charge cascading, (LC4) a seismic lateral equivalent of 0.15 g on the full mill mass. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) peak soil bearing pressure must not exceed 500 kPa at the foundation-soil interface, (R2) ACI 318-19 rebar DCR must remain at or below 1.0 under factored combinations, (R3) pedestal top velocity at 0.158 Hz must remain below 10 mm/s rms per DIN 4150-3 Class II, (R4) the first natural frequency of the pedestal-soil system must be at least 3 Hz to clear the 0.158 Hz forcing frequency by a factor of 10, and (R5) maximum tilt of the bearing seat under static loads must not exceed 0.5 mm/m to protect the trunnion bearing alignment. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Vibration analysis is required because LC3 is a harmonic load. Deliver a STEP AP242 solid body of the pedestal with rebar cage sub-bodies plus a foundation report listing bearing pressure, rebar DCR, vibration velocity, first natural frequency, and static tilt.
short_prompt:
Design a reinforced concrete trunnion bearing pedestal for the feed-end of a 10.4 m diameter x 5.2 m long gearless SAG mill (hard-rock copper, 60000 t/day, 1250 t operating mass charged with 40% ball filling, 12.5 MW drive, 9.5 rpm = 0.158 Hz). The pedestal is a 5.0 m long x 4.0 m wide x 3.0 m tall normal-weight concrete block (f'c 34.5 MPa, ASTM A615 Grade 60 rebar). Design per ACI 318-19 with DIN 4150-3 Class II vibration limits. Vibration analysis is required because LC3 is harmonic.

Load cases.
- LC1 vertical static: 6130 kN bearing reaction at the feed-end.
- LC2 horizontal static torque: 1550 kN from drive torque transmitted through the mill shell.
- LC3 charge-cascading harmonic: 400 kN vertical + 200 kN horizontal at 0.158 Hz.
- LC4 seismic: 0.15 g lateral equivalent on the full mill mass.
Peak soil bearing pressure under factored combinations <= 500 kPa, rebar DCR <= 1.0, pedestal-top velocity at 0.158 Hz <= 10 mm/s rms, first pedestal-soil natural frequency >= 3 Hz (10x clearance of 0.158 Hz), and bearing-seat tilt under static loads <= 0.5 mm/m.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Charge cascading force amplitudes on SAG mill trunnions are derived from mill-charge ballistic models (Morrell/Powell), not from structural FEA.

prompt.title: 10.4 m SAG mill feed-end trunnion pedestal
prompt.brief: Design a 5.0 x 4.0 x 3.0 m RC pedestal for the feed-end trunnion of a 10.4 m x 5.2 m SAG mill processing 60,000 t/d copper ore, carrying 6.13 MN vertical, 1.55 MN horizontal, and 0.158 Hz charge dynamics, meeting bearing, ACI, vibration, and alignment limits.

boundary_conditions.soil: Winkler bed with subgrade modulus 60 MPa per hard-rock site

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: solid concrete with embedded rebar, Winkler soil

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Mill charge dynamics supplied as force amplitudes.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Gearless motor drive (GMD) electromagnetic harmonic content

eval_coverage: mining mill machine foundation structural + vibration stack
